title: Protein & Fibre Flapjacks
summary: These no-bake protein flapjacks are the perfect grab-and-go snack. Packed with seeds, nut butter, and protein powder, they’re ideal for a post-workout refuel or a midday energy boost. They take just 20 minutes to prep and can be customised however you fancy.

method.description: Add the oats, protein powder, peanut butter, almond milk, flaxseeds, chia seeds, and mixed seeds to a large bowl. Mix everything together until you have a thick, cookie dough-like paste.
Line a small baking tray with parchment paper. Press the mixture firmly into the tray, smoothing the top with the back of a spoon.
Refrigerate for at least 3 hours, or until firm. Once chilled, slice into 6 bars or squares.
Eat as-is or jazz them up with your favourite toppings—try a dollop of yoghurt, some fresh berries, or a drizzle of melted dark chocolate and tahini.

tips: To store: Keep flapjacks in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 1 week.
To freeze: Freeze individually wrapped portions for up to 2 months. Defrost overnight in the fridge before eating.
Meal prep tip: Double the batch and freeze half so you always have a protein-packed snack on hand.
Vanilla whey protein → Use plant-based protein powder for a vegan version.
Almond milk → Swap for oat or soya milk.
Peanut butter → Use almond or cashew butter for a different flavour profile.
Mixed seeds → Use whatever seeds you have on hand—pumpkin, sunflower, hemp, or sesame all work well.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
